candidates.title: CRA: FM1 Spectral Diagnosis + Systematic Benchmark + Task-Type Boundary
candidates.summary: Lead with empirical contributions: (1) First direct measurement of gradient covariance effective rank at LLM scale showing r_eff << d << B; (2) First systematic 2x2 factorial on DATE-LM with both rank and continuous metrics; (3) Task-type boundary discovery (semantic vs behavioral attribution); (4) phi^T M psi as organizational taxonomy. FM2 validation and PCA whitening as conditional extensions depending on Priority 1 and 5 outcomes.
hypotheses: H1: RepSim > TRAK on semantic tasks (+15pp counterfact, +10pp ftrace); reversed on toxicity
H4-rev: Gradient r_eff in O(10-100), confirming extreme signal concentration
H5-rev: TRAK saturates at k << d (around d/8 to d/4)
H10: 30.8pp TRAK-PCA gap decomposes into layer mixing + cosine normalization + semantic features
H11: Controlled FM2 injection degrades performance; contrastive scoring recovers it
H2-rev: Continuous metrics detect FM2 asymmetry (param > rep improvement)
H7-rev: PCA-reduced whitening outperforms M=I at k=64-128 where N/k >> 40
candidates.pilot_focus: Pilot complete. All 14 tasks executed. FM1 strongly supported, FM2 untested, H7/H9 failed at pilot scale. Ready for full-scale.
key_risks: Retrieval models match RepSim -> reposition as attribution-vs-retrieval boundary paper
FM2 undetectable even with continuous metrics -> narrow to FM1-only + benchmark paper
PCA whitening fails at all k -> framework is taxonomic not prescriptive
30.8pp gap doesn't decompose -> 'feature quality not dimensionality' dominates

evidence_status.whitened_attribution: failed at pilot scale, PCA reduction planned
evidence_status.framework_predictive: weak (3/3 distinctive predictions failed or trivial)

candidates.title: Hessian Quality Diagnosis Paper
candidates.summary: K-FAC IF control experiment (H6) confirmed: RepSim > K-FAC IF by 17.4pp on counterfact. FM1 is independent of Hessian quality. This pivot path is closed.
candidates.drop_reason: H6 confirmed at pilot scale. Even with high-quality Hessian (K-FAC full eigendecomp), parameter-space IF still fails vs RepSim by large margin.

candidates.title: Matched Filter Theory for Optimal Data Attribution
candidates.summary: If PCA-reduced whitened attribution (H7-revised) succeeds at full scale (+3pp or more), elevate the signal processing theory to a primary contribution. Derive optimal M from detection theory, provide per-query SNR reliability estimates.
hypotheses: H7-rev: PCA-whitened RepSim outperforms standard RepSim by 3-5pp at k=64-128
Per-query SNR_out predicts attribution accuracy (r > 0.3, pilot showed r=0.34)
Whitening improvement correlates with representation anisotropy (Prop 2.2)
candidates.pilot_focus: PCA whitening at full scale (Priority 5 experiment)
key_risks: Covariance estimation still marginal even with PCA reduction
SNR correlation may not generalize beyond pilot
Narrower scope than cand_a if FM1 spectral evidence is the stronger story
candidates.promotion_criteria: PCA-whitened attribution at any k outperforms RepSim by >= 5pp on >= 2 tasks

candidates.title: Attribution vs Retrieval: When Does Model-Internal Information Matter?
candidates.summary: If retrieval baselines (Contriever/GTR) match RepSim on semantic tasks, the paper pivots to characterizing the boundary between attribution (model-internal) and retrieval (model-external) quality. Toxicity reversal becomes the anchor: it is the clearest case where model-internal gradient information provides genuine attribution signal that retrieval cannot capture.
hypotheses: H8-rev: Retrieval models match RepSim on counterfact/ftrace but fail on toxicity
Gradient norm directly correlates with toxicity (behavioral signal)
Semantic similarity is necessary but not sufficient for attribution on behavioral tasks
key_risks: May be seen as a negative result paper rather than a contribution
Toxicity reversal alone may be insufficient for a full paper
candidates.promotion_criteria: Contriever/GTR < 3pp gap with RepSim on 2+ tasks

decision_criteria.promote_cand_c: PCA-whitened attribution at any k outperforms RepSim by >= 5pp on >= 2 tasks
decision_criteria.promote_cand_d: Contriever/GTR achieves < 3pp gap with RepSim on 2+ tasks
decision_criteria.drop_cand_c: PCA-whitened attribution fails to outperform RepSim by >= 3pp at any k on any task
decision_criteria.drop_cand_d: RepSim > Contriever/GTR by >= 10pp on 2+ tasks (attribution clearly differs from retrieval)

metadata.pilot_summary: GO with narrative revisions. FM1 strongly supported (r_eff=10, spectral concentration). FM2 untested (eval protocol failure). H7 failed (N/d=0.049). H9 falsified (direction reversed). Toxicity reversal defines task-type boundary.
metadata.synthesizer_reasoning: Front-runner restructured from theory-first to evidence-first approach based on pilot outcomes. Key shifts: (1) FM1 spectral diagnosis is the strongest empirical pillar -- lead with it. (2) FM2 needs causal validation via controlled injection before any claims. (3) phi^T M psi is taxonomic until H7-revised succeeds at full scale. (4) Task-type boundary (toxicity reversal) is a genuine new contribution, not a failure. (5) Contrarian's 'RepSim is retrieval' challenge must be addressed via retrieval baselines before claiming attribution quality. Empiricist (9.0), Pragmatist (8.5), and Contrarian (8.5) weighted most heavily due to superior pilot evidence interpretation.
